rooms.name: The Entry Foyer
rooms.description: You wake on the cold marble of Thornwick Library's foyer. The front door is sealed by an electromagnetic lock. A wall clock above the reception desk is stopped at 11:47. A welcome mat curls at your feet; a battered umbrella stand leans against the wall. There are 3 brass nails set into the door frame.

rooms.name: The Reading Hall
rooms.description: Eight tall bookshelves climb to a vaulted ceiling. A librarian's rolling cart sits half-shelved beside the 800-section. A glass display case glints in the lamplight, locked with a three-digit dial. A fireplace yawns cold against the north wall; the iron grate is loose.

rooms.name: The Archives
rooms.description: Steel filing cabinets line the walls in 4 long rows. A brass cipher wheel is bolted to the south wall, its outer ring etched with letters. An iron chest sits dead-center, its lid stamped with a single keyhole. The pneumatic-tube terminal hisses softly in the corner, its tube empty and waiting. The manuscript pedestal stands bare.

items.name: Desk Key
items.description: A small brass key, warm to the touch. Fits a desk drawer.

items.name: Brass Magnifying Glass
items.description: Heavier than it looks. The lens is etched with hairline grid lines.

items.name: Scrap of Paper
items.description: A charred scrap, partially legible: '...the second digit is 8...'

items.name: Torn Manuscript Page
items.description: A handwritten page in old library script. It names a thief.

items.name: Evidence Token
items.description: Proof transmitted. The lock is open. You won.

puzzles.description: A riddle is carved into the clock frame.
puzzles.questionText: The clock's hands are frozen. The frame reads: 'When does the library's silence begin? Answer in HH:MM.'

puzzles.description: A three-dial combination lock guards the display case. The digits are hidden in the room descriptions: 3 brass nails in the foyer, 8 bookshelves in the reading hall, 4 rows of filing cabinets in the archives.
Six books from the librarian's cart must be reshelved in Dewey Decimal order. Smallest number first.
The cipher wheel's letters are too small to read by lamplight alone.
puzzles.outcomeMessage: Under the lens, etched letters resolve into a single word: THORNWICK.

puzzles.description: The iron chest demands a passphrase.
puzzles.questionText: The chest's brass plate reads: 'Speak the name of this place to open me.'

puzzles.description: The pneumatic-tube terminal waits for evidence to transmit.
puzzles.outcomeMessage: The tube hisses. Evidence received. The front door's electromagnetic lock disengages. You are free.
